prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetie, a CPU is like the "brain" of a computer. It's a small, square chip that sits on the computer's motherboard and does most of the thinking and processing. When you use a program or run a game, the CPU figures out what needs to be done and makes it happen. It's very fast and smart, even if I don't fully understand all the technical ins and outs. Just remember, without the CPU, your computer would be like a dummy box that can't do anything at all!
